design_philosophy.core_loop: Switch between robot ground mode and jet flight mode to navigate terrain, manage fuel, collect power-ups, and defeat enemies using two weapon types (Pulse Laser and charged Big-Bang cannon)
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource management (fuel depletion during flight) combined with transformation vulnerability windows—player must choose when to expose themselves during mode switches to progress through level geometry
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Pilot a transformable robot-jet hybrid capable of adapting to different environmental challenges (land, ocean, space) and deploying devastating charged weapons against powerful bosses
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Enforces a specific dual-mode gameplay paradigm where transformation is mandatory for progression but creates tactical vulnerability. Big-Bang charging mechanic is required for boss progression, forcing players into a rhythmic gameplay pattern. No optional difficulty adjustment—single vision of how shmup challenges should scale

difficulty.lives_system: Single hit death with lives counter. Player loses life when robot/jet is hit or when jet crashes due to fuel depletion or altitude miscalculation
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing. Game restarts from Earth portion on loop completion with modified enemy patterns, suggesting arcade-style continue structure typical of era
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No explicit difficulty settings. Player agency comes from mastering transformation timing, fuel management precision, and weapon charging rhythm. Altitude awareness system provides skill expression window

progression.upgrade_system: Two-tier weapon system: Pulse Laser (standard, always available) and Big-Bang (charged, stronger, multi-target). Big-Bang is mandatory for boss defeat. No persistent character upgrades across loops
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression gates by geography and required mode transformations. Fuel orbs (white spheres) are only collectable item type. Ocean sections force jet transformation. Boss encounters gate access to space section
replayability_hooks: Endless loop structure with enemy pattern variations on repeat playthroughs
Two distinct environmental sections (Earth: grassland/desert, Space) provide varied terrain challenges

world_building_techniques: Two-act structure: Earth battle (mountainous grassland + Egyptian desert) transitioning to outer space alien confrontation
Environmental geography forces narrative progression (ocean crossing requires jet, space battle requires environmental transition)
Simple thematic escalation: terrestrial threat → alien superweapon in space

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters mandate mode-specific strategies and charged weapon usage. Both bosses (STV on Earth, alien superweapon in space) require Big-Bang cannon to defeat. Bosses engage during aerial phases, eliminating fuel consumption concern during critical battles
notable_bosses: STV (Earth boss, ocean location, requires Big-Bang to defeat)
Alien Superweapon (Space boss, final confrontation, no fuel depletion threat during fight)

faction_indicators: Earth defenders vs. alien invaders (implicit two-faction conflict)
Robot Ixpel as protagonist ally against external alien threat
danger_signals: Alien superweapon as existential threat requiring final confrontation
environmental_storytelling: Progression from peaceful grasslands to hostile desert to desolate space represents escalating threat levels
Ocean crossing as natural barrier requiring technological adaptation
Space environment as alien domain visually distinct from Earth sections
